heading: Exceptions for national security and humanitarian assistance; rule of construction
label: 9410 Exceptions for national security and humanitarian assistance; rule of construction
notes: References in TextThe National Security Act of 1947, referred to in subsec. (a)(1), is , . children.text: Any activity subject to the reporting requirements under title V of the National Security Act of 1947 ( et seq.), or to any authorized intelligence activities of the United States.50 U.S.C. 3091

The admission of an alien to the United States if such admission is necessary to comply with United States obligations under the Agreement between the United Nations and the United States of America regarding the Headquarters of the United Nations, signed at Lake Success , and entered into force , or under the Convention on Consular Relations, done at Vienna , and entered into force , or other applicable international obligations of the United States.June 26, 1947November 21, 1947April 24, 1963March 19, 1967

The conduct or facilitation of a transaction for the sale of agricultural commodities, food, medicine, or medical devices to Iran or for the provision of humanitarian assistance to the people of Iran, including engaging in a financial transaction relating to humanitarian assistance or for humanitarian purposes or transporting goods or services that are necessary to carry out operations relating to humanitarian assistance or humanitarian purposes.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: b Implementation
children.text: The President may exercise all authorities provided under sections 203 and 205 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( and 1704) to carry out this chapter.50 U.S.C. 1702

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: c Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to limit the authority of the President under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.).50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Agricultural commodity
children.label: 1 Agricultural commodity
children.text: The term “agricultural commodity” has the meaning given that term in .section 5602 of title 7

children.heading: Good
children.label: 2 Good
children.text: The term “good” has the meaning given that term in section 4618 of title 50 (as continued in effect pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.)).150 U.S.C. 1701See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Medical device
children.label: 3 Medical device
children.text: The term “medical device” has the meaning given the term “device” in .section 321 of title 21

children.heading: Medicine
children.label: 4 Medicine
children.text: The term “medicine” has the meaning given the term “drug” in .section 321 of title 21
